sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन् नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin nāyaṃ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ | ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṃ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāṇe śarīre ||
literal_meaning: Never born, never dies; having not been, will not again be; unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient; not slain when the body is slain.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s panic by pointing to an indestructible core-self that transcends the life-cycle of the body, dissolving the fear of annihilation that fuels avoidance.
psychological_state: Acute death-anxiety and ego-dissolution panic; needs cognitive re-anchoring in a non-physical, timeless identity to restore executive function.
trigger_scenario: A soldier with PTSD before deployment, a patient receiving a terminal diagnosis, or anyone facing sudden bereavement—moments when the continuity of personal existence feels shattered.
anchor_text: You are the deathless witness within the perishable body.
